DomainSummaries.description: A list containing details about each domain in the Amazon Web Services account.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the domain.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain.

ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration.description: The server-side encryption configuration containing the KMS key identifier you want Voice ID to use to encrypt your data.

ServerSideEncryptionUpdateDetails.description: Details about the most recent server-side encryption configuration update. When the server-side encryption configuration is changed, dependency on the old KMS key is removed through an asynchronous process. When this update is complete, the domain's data can only be accessed using the new KMS key.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was last updated.

Fraudster.description: Contains all the information about a fraudster.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when Voice ID identified the fraudster.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster.

WatchlistIds.description: The identifier of the watchlists the fraudster is a part of.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the domain.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain.

ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration.description: The server-side encryption configuration containing the KMS key identifier you want Voice ID to use to encrypt your data.

ServerSideEncryptionUpdateDetails.description: Details about the most recent server-side encryption configuration update. When the server-side encryption configuration is changed, dependency on the old KMS key is removed through an asynchronous process. When this update is complete, the domain's data can only be accessed using the new KMS key.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was last update.

WatchlistDetails.description: The watchlist details of a domain. Contains the default watchlist ID of the domain.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was created.

DefaultWatchlist.description: Whether the specified watchlist is the default watchlist of a domain.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the watchlist.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was updated.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the domain.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain.

ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration.description: The server-side encryption configuration containing the KMS key identifier you want Voice ID to use to encrypt your data.

ServerSideEncryptionUpdateDetails.description: Details about the most recent server-side encryption configuration update. When the server-side encryption configuration is changed, dependency on the old KMS key is removed through an asynchronous process. When this update is complete, the domain's data can only be accessed using the new KMS key.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the domain was last update.

WatchlistDetails.description: The watchlist details of a domain. Contains the default watchlist ID of the domain.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when Voice ID identified the fraudster.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster.

WatchlistIds.description: The identifier of the watchlists the fraudster is a part of.

Job.description: Contains details about the specified fraudster registration job.

CreatedAt.description: A timestamp of when the fraudster registration job was created.

DataAccessRoleArn.description: The IAM role Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that grants Voice ID permissions to access customer's buckets to read the input manifest file and write the job output file.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster registration job.

EndedAt.description: A timestamp of when the fraudster registration job ended.

FailureDetails.description: Contains details that are populated when an entire batch job fails. In cases of individual registration job failures, the batch job as a whole doesn't fail; it is completed with a JobStatus of COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS. You can use the job output file to identify the individual registration requests that failed.

InputDataConfig.description: The input data config containing an S3 URI for the input manifest file that contains the list of fraudster registration job requests.

JobId.description: The service-generated identifier for the fraudster registration job.

JobName.description: The client-provided name for the fraudster registration job.

JobProgress.description: Shows the completed percentage of registration requests listed in the input file.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the fraudster registration job.

OutputDataConfig.description: The output data config containing the S3 location where you want Voice ID to write your job output file; you must also include a KMS key ID in order to encrypt the file.

RegistrationConfig.description: The registration config containing details such as the action to take when a duplicate fraudster is detected, and the similarity threshold to use for detecting a duplicate fraudster.

CreatedAt.description: A timestamp of when the speaker was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the speaker.

LastAccessedAt.description: The timestamp of when the speaker was last accessed for enrollment, re-enrollment or a successful authentication. This timestamp is accurate to one hour.

UpdatedAt.description: A timestamp of the speaker's last update.

Job.description: Contains details about the specified speaker enrollment job.

CreatedAt.description: A timestamp of when the speaker enrollment job was created.

DataAccessRoleArn.description: The IAM role Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that grants Voice ID permissions to access customer's buckets to read the input manifest file and write the job output file.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the speaker enrollment job.

EndedAt.description: A timestamp of when the speaker enrollment job ended.

EnrollmentConfig.description: The configuration that defines the action to take when the speaker is already enrolled in Voice ID, and the FraudDetectionConfig to use.

FailureDetails.description: Contains details that are populated when an entire batch job fails. In cases of individual registration job failures, the batch job as a whole doesn't fail; it is completed with a JobStatus of COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS. You can use the job output file to identify the individual registration requests that failed.

InputDataConfig.description: The input data config containing an S3 URI for the input manifest file that contains the list of speaker enrollment job requests.

JobId.description: The service-generated identifier for the speaker enrollment job.

JobName.description: The client-provided name for the speaker enrollment job.

JobProgress.description: Provides details on job progress. This field shows the completed percentage of registration requests listed in the input file.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the speaker enrollment job.

OutputDataConfig.description: The output data config containing the S3 location where Voice ID writes the job output file; you must also include a KMS key ID to encrypt the file.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was created.

DefaultWatchlist.description: Whether the specified watchlist is the default watchlist of a domain.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the watchlist.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was updated.

Fraudster.description: Contains all the information about a fraudster.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when Voice ID identified the fraudster.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster.

WatchlistIds.description: The identifier of the watchlists the fraudster is a part of.

AuthenticationResult.description: Details resulting from the authentication process, such as authentication decision and authentication score.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the session.

FraudDetectionResult.description: Details resulting from the fraud detection process, such as fraud detection decision and risk score.

StreamingStatus.description: The current status of audio streaming for this session. This field is useful to infer next steps when the Authentication or Fraud Detection results are empty or the decision is NOT_ENOUGH_SPEECH. In this situation, if the StreamingStatus is ONGOING/PENDING_CONFIGURATION, it can mean that the client should call the API again later, after Voice ID has enough audio to produce a result. If the decision remains NOT_ENOUGH_SPEECH even after StreamingStatus is ENDED, it means that the previously streamed session did not have enough speech to perform evaluation, and a new streaming session is needed to try again.

AudioAggregationEndedAt.description: A timestamp of when audio aggregation ended for this authentication result.

AudioAggregationStartedAt.description: A timestamp of when audio aggregation started for this authentication result.

AuthenticationResultId.description: The unique identifier for this authentication result. Because there can be multiple authentications for a given session, this field helps to identify if the returned result is from a previous streaming activity or a new result. Note that in absence of any new streaming activity, AcceptanceThreshold changes, or SpeakerId changes, Voice ID always returns cached Authentication Result for this API.

Configuration.description: The AuthenticationConfiguration used to generate this authentication result.

CustomerSpeakerId.description: The client-provided identifier for the speaker whose authentication result is produced. Only present if a SpeakerId is provided for the session.

Decision.description: The authentication decision produced by Voice ID, processed against the current session state and streamed audio of the speaker.

GeneratedSpeakerId.description: The service-generated identifier for the speaker whose authentication result is produced.

Score.description: The authentication score for the speaker whose authentication result is produced. This value is only present if the authentication decision is either ACCEPT or REJECT.

JobSummaries.description: A list containing details about each specified fraudster registration job.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster registration job.

EndedAt.description: A timestamp of when the fraudster registration job ended.

FailureDetails.description: Contains details that are populated when an entire batch job fails. In cases of individual registration job failures, the batch job as a whole doesn't fail; it is completed with a JobStatus of COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS. You can use the job output file to identify the individual registration requests that failed.

JobId.description: The service-generated identifier for the fraudster registration job.

JobName.description: The client-provided name for the fraudster registration job.

JobProgress.description: Shows the completed percentage of registration requests listed in the input file.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the fraudster registration job.

FraudsterSummaries.description: A list that contains details about each fraudster in the Amazon Web Services account.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the fraudster summary was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the fraudster summary.

WatchlistIds.description: The identifier of the watchlists the fraudster is a part of.

JobSummaries.description: A list containing details about each specified speaker enrollment job.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

CreatedAt.description: A timestamp of when of the speaker enrollment job was created.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the speaker enrollment job.

EndedAt.description: A timestamp of when the speaker enrollment job ended.

FailureDetails.description: Contains details that are populated when an entire batch job fails. In cases of individual registration job failures, the batch job as a whole doesn't fail; it is completed with a JobStatus of COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS. You can use the job output file to identify the individual registration requests that failed.

JobId.description: The service-generated identifier for the speaker enrollment job.

JobName.description: The client-provided name for the speaker enrollment job.

JobProgress.description: Provides details regarding job progress. This field shows the completed percentage of enrollment requests listed in the input file.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the speaker enrollment job.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

SpeakerSummaries.description: A list containing details about each speaker in the Amazon Web Services account.

CreatedAt.description: A timestamp showing the speaker's creation time.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the speaker.

LastAccessedAt.description: The timestamp when the speaker was last accessed for enrollment, re-enrollment or a successful authentication. This timestamp is accurate to one hour.

UpdatedAt.description: A timestamp showing the speaker's last update.

Tags.description: The list of tags associated with the specified resource.

Key.description: The first part of a key:value pair that forms a tag associated with a given resource. For example, in the tag 'Department':'Sales', the key is 'Department'.

Value.description: The second part of a key:value pair that forms a tag associated with a given resource. For example, in the tag 'Department':'Sales', the value is 'Sales'.

NextToken.description: If NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was created.

DefaultWatchlist.description: Whether the specified watchlist is the default watchlist of a domain.

DomainId.description: The identifier of the domain that contains the watchlist.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the watchlist was last updated.
